titleStmt.title: SII 22.38: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 38. (A.R. No. 38 of 1906) TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR. AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL. TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA III—9th YEAR. (A.D. 1186-7)

summary.p: The king claims to have taken Madurai. This records yet another gift of two perpetul lamps by the reclamation of 1400 kuḻi of land in the same village by one Arayaṉ Tiruvanantīśaṉ alias Kēraḷarāyan of Perunallūr in Kīḻvēṅgai-nāḍu a division of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . tiripuvanaccakkaravarttikaḷ maturai koṇṭaruḷiṉa śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu Oṉpatāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu Ākkūrnāṭṭu tirukkaṭavūr Uṭaiyār śrīkālakālad[eva]r koyilil tirununtā viḷakku
Eriya rājarājavaḷanāṭṭu kiḻveṅkaināṭṭu perunallūru(u)ṭaiyāṉ Arayaṉ tiruvaṉanticanaārāṉa keraḷarāyaṉ Ittevar tevatāṉam Erukkāṭṭuc ceriyil maṇal kuṉṟil viḷainilam kalliṉa kuḻi 1[4 100] Āyirattunāṉūṟṟināl nilam Araiye nālu
māvukku nāḷ Oṉṟukku ney Uḻakkāka Oṉpatāvatu puraṭṭāti māsam piṟanta Irupattuañcāntiyatimutal cantirātittavarai Eriya Ēṟiṉa tirununtā viḷakku Iraṇṭu .
Digital edition of SII 22.38 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
